title: Narcissus humilis
taxonomy: Narcissus humilis belongs to the family Amaryllidaceae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales. The genus Narcissus is a group of bulbous perennial plants, and Narcissus humilis is a species within this group. The Amaryllidaceae family includes various flowering plants, primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere. Narcissus humilis is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Western Asia.
uses: Narcissus humilis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities, which may contribute to its potential therapeutic applications. In traditional medicine, Narcissus humilis has been used to treat various health conditions, including infections, wounds, and respiratory issues. Its bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and phenolic acids, are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully explore the potential benefits of using Narcissus humilis as a medicinal plant.

intro: Narcissus humilis, also known as the lesser jonquil or dwarf daffodil, is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus humilis include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, particularly narciclasine and homonarciclasine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Narcissus humilis is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried flower extract, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections, digestive issues, and nervous system disorders.
